cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about the actions of a search group formed by American scientists in 1975. The options are A to D. First, I need to recall the relevant part of the text. The text says that in 1975, some American scientists formed a search group. They used an underwater camera that took pictures every seventy seconds. Some of the pictures seemed to show a red-brown creature with a four-meter body and a four-meter neck. This made many people believe in the monster, but even today, we can't be certain. Looking at the options: A) found the monster itself. But the text says they took pictures that seemed to show a creature, but they didn't actually find the monster. It's still uncertain. So A is incorrect. B) found a huge cave underwater. Wait, that was mentioned in an earlier part. The text says that later, underwater TV cameras found a huge underwater cave, but that was before 1975. The 1975 group used a camera to take pictures, not to find the cave. So B is not correct for the 1975 group. C) believed there wasn't any monster. The text says many people began to believe in the monster after the 1975 pictures. The scientists' actions made people believe, but the text doesn't say the scientists themselves believed there wasn't a monster. So C is probably incorrect. D)
